title: Attendance Past 7 Days Report SOP
author: Retention Team
summary: Pipeline Party preparation process for pulling the Clubworx attendance report, grouping members by recent attendance, and identifying low-engagement members for follow-up.
content:
## Process
Attendance Past 7 Days Report (Pipeline Party)

## What
Preparing the Attendance Past 7 Days report from Clubworx and organising members into attendance groups for use in Pipeline Party.

## Why
This report shows who is training and who is slipping. It allows the team to identify low engagement early, prioritise follow-ups, and drive retention through targeted action.

## When
Every Monday before the Pipeline Party meeting.

## Who
Reception / Admin.

## Step 1 - Pull & Set Up Report
- Download the Attendance Report from Clubworx.
- Open the report in Google Sheets.
- Add today's date at the top of the sheet.

## Step 2 - Sort Attendance Data
- Select the entire sheet.
- Click Data > Sort Range > Advanced Range Sorting Options.
- Tick "Data has header row".
- Sort by Attendances Last 7 Days.
- Confirm the list is now ordered from lowest to highest attendance.

## Step 3 - Split Members Into Attendance Groups
- Create new tabs by pressing the "+" button.
- In the main sheet, select all members with 0 attendances.
- Copy and paste them into a new tab named "0".
- Repeat for each attendance level: 1 attendance into tab "1", 2 attendances into tab "2", and continue for all attendance levels up to 4+.

## Step 4 - Clean Each Tab
- In each tab, select all data.
- Click Data > Sort Range > Advanced Options.
- Tick "Data has header row".
- Sort by Membership Name.

## Step 5 - Remove Non-Relevant Members
- Identify all Kickstarter members. Highlight and delete these rows because they are managed separately.
- Identify any Thailand Tour, Sponsored, or Payment-type memberships. Highlight and delete these rows because they are not relevant to attendance tracking.

## Step 6 - Finalise Numbers
- At the bottom of each tab, add the total number of members.
- Subtract 1 from the total to exclude the header row.

## Step 7 - Final Checks
- Confirm all tabs are correctly labelled: 0, 1, 2, etc.
- Confirm all data is sorted by Membership Name.
- Confirm all irrelevant members have been removed.
- Confirm totals are accurate on each tab.
- Confirm the sheet is shared and accessible.

## Step 8 - Share With The Team
- Click Share and give access to miami@urbanmuythai.com, getfit@urbanmuythai.com, and melissa@urbanmuaythai.com.
- Ensure the sheet is accessible before the Pipeline Party meeting begins.

## Key Insight
This report is used during Pipeline Party to identify low-attendance members and support Fringe Reach-Outs. It shows who is slipping before they disappear. The team uses it to catch low engagement early, step in, and keep members training.

## Standard Of Execution
- Sheet is clean, structured, and easy to navigate.
- Tabs are correctly split and labelled: 0, 1, 2, etc.
- No irrelevant members are included.
- Totals are accurate on each tab.
- Sheet is shared and ready before Pipeline Party.

## Key Principle
This report shows who is slipping before they disappear. The team uses it to catch low engagement early, step in, and keep members training.

## Source Document
Google Doc: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1vh6M5L_z_YKYxXCdmFKIF6gFFtPGDnGX/edit

title: Morning Opening Checklist
author: Operations Team
summary: Step-by-step procedure for opening the gym each morning safely and efficiently.
content:
## Purpose
Ensure the facility opens consistently, safely, and on time every morning.

## Responsible Staff
Opening manager or designated team lead.

## Steps
1. **Arrive 15 minutes early** - Collect keys from the key safe.
2. **Perimeter check** - Walk the exterior and check for damage or security concerns.
3. **Disable alarm** - Disarm the security system within 30 seconds of entry.
4. **Lights & ventilation** - Turn on all lighting and set HVAC to 20C.
5. **Equipment check** - Power on all bags, pads, and cardio equipment.
6. **Facilities check** - Inspect change rooms and restock towels and amenities.
7. **Reception setup** - Log into the booking system and confirm class schedule.
8. **Open doors** - Unlock main entrance at scheduled open time.

title: Evening Closing Procedure
author: Operations Team
summary: End-of-day closing checklist including security and lockup.
content:
## Purpose
Ensure the gym is closed securely and all end-of-day tasks are completed.

## Steps
1. Announce closing 15 minutes before close time.
2. Ensure all members have exited and change rooms are cleared.
3. Complete end-of-day sales and attendance report.
4. Wipe down and store all pads and equipment.
5. Final walkthrough - check all rooms and fire exits.
6. Set the alarm and exit within 30 seconds.
7. Lock all exterior doors and test each one.
8. Complete the closing log.

title: Class Delivery Protocol
author: Head Instructor
summary: Standards for delivering a safe and high-quality Muay Thai class.
content:
## Before Class
- Arrive 10 minutes before start time
- Inspect training area for hazards
- Confirm attendee list in booking system
- Set up pads and equipment

## During Class
- Begin with a structured warm-up
- Clearly explain and demonstrate all techniques
- Monitor students for safety and correct form
- Maintain a class register for insurance and safety purposes

title: New Staff Orientation
author: HR & Ops
summary: First-day and first-week checklist for all new team members.
content:
## Day 1 Checklist
- Issue access card and assign locker
- Complete WHS induction
- Set up booking platform, email, and POS access
- Walk through facility and introduce the team
- Review key SOPs: Opening, Closing, Emergency

title: Equipment Maintenance Log
author: Facilities
summary: Monthly equipment inspection checklist and fault reporting process.
content:
## Monthly Inspection
All equipment must be inspected on the first Monday of each month.

## Checklist
- Inspect all heavy bags for tears and chain wear
- Check all striking pads for damage
- Test emergency stops on cardio equipment
- Inspect floor mats for tears or trip hazards
- Check first aid kit is fully stocked

title: Member Complaint Resolution
author: Customer Ops
summary: Standard approach to handling member complaints professionally.
content:
## Guiding Principles
All complaints must be treated with respect, resolved promptly, and documented.

## Steps
1. **Listen** - Let the member speak without interruption.
2. **Acknowledge** - Validate their concern genuinely.
3. **Clarify** - Ask questions to fully understand the issue.
4. **Resolve** - Offer a practical solution within your authority.
5. **Document** - Log the complaint and outcome in the CRM.
6. **Follow up** - Contact the member within 48 hours.
